title: Free Rook on d5
setup: Black's rook stands alone on d5 — no defender in sight.
hint: The knight reaches d5 in one hop. Is anything defending the rook?
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a **loose piece** capture — the simplest tactic of all. The knight on c3 can leap to d5 (file +1, rank +2 — a textbook L-shape) and grab the rook. Black's only piece left is the king on e8, which is three squares away from d5 and cannot defend it.

After {{Nxd5}}, White is up a clean rook for nothing.

## Why It Works

- The rook is **undefended** — always check whether a target piece has a guard before assuming it's free.
- The knight on c3 reaches d5 in one move via the L-shape (c3 → d5).
- White's own king on e1 isn't in the way and isn't in any danger.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Before every move, scan the board for **loose pieces** — enemy pieces with no defender. They are gifts waiting to be collected.
